articles.title: Dollar Rallies on a Hot US PPI Report
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rallied to a 1.5-week high on Wednesday, finishing up by 0.22%. The dollar moved higher on Wednesday on the stronger-than-expected US Apr PPI report, which was hawkish for Fed policy. The dollar also has safe-haven support on concerns that the US-Iran ceasefire may break down...

articles.title: British Pound: Political uncertainty offsets growth support – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lee Hardman explains that the Pound (GBP) is softer despite stronger-than-expected United Kingdom (UK) Gross Domestic Product (GDP), as markets anticipate slower growth later in the year due to the energy price shock.

articles.title: KC Fed Pres Schmid: Inflation remains too high.
articles.expand: KC Fed Pres Schmid is speaking and says: Continued inflation is the most pressing risk to the economy It is clear inflation remains too high US economy has shown “remarkable resilience” and economic fundamentals remain sound Higher oil prices still drain household spending power and raise business costs US economy less vulnerable to global oil disr...

articles.title: USDCAD buyers are taking the price above the 100 day MA. Buyers make a play.
articles.expand: The USDCAD is breaking above its 100-day moving average at 1.3718, a key technical level that has repeatedly capped rallies over the past month. Recall that on Tuesday the pair briefly moved above that moving average, only to quickly reverse lower. Yesterday, sellers once again leaned against the same level and stalled the advance. Today, however, ...

articles.expand: Prior was +2.0% (revised to +2.4%) Wholesale sales (excluding petroleum, petroleum products, and other hydrocarbons and excluding oilseed and grain) rose 1.9% to $89.0 billion in March. Sales increased in five of the seven subsectors, representing 79.6% of total wholesale sales. The largest increase came from the machinery, equipment and supplies s...

articles.title: Japanese Yen steadies as strong US Dollar offsets hawkish BoJ signals
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades around 157.95 on Thursday at the time of writing, up modestly by 0.05% on the day, as the pair remains close to its highest level in two weeks.

articles.title: Gold holds firm despite hawkish Fed repricing boosts Treasury yields, US Dollar
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) holds firm on Thursday, remaining confined within a week-old trading range as investors closely monitor a high-stakes meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, while US-Iran negotiations remain in limbo.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Higher yields support prospects for BoJ hike – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lee Hardman notes that rising long-term JGB yields, driven by inflation risks linked to the Middle East conflict and hawkish BoJ commentary, are reinforcing expectations for a near-term policy rate increase.

articles.title: NZD/USD Signal: Can the Kiwi Hold 0.59 Support Before US Non-Farm Payrolls? - 14 May 2026
articles.expand: NZD/USD is consolidating near 0.5950, with 0.59 as a potential buy zone and 0.60 as the key upside resistance ahead of US jobs data.

articles.title: US Dollar Threatening to Break Higher
articles.expand: The US dollar is trading quietly against the major currencies. The euro is holding above $1.17 and sterling is holding above $1.35, but the market does not appear done probing these support areas. The greenback has also traded as close to JPY158 as possible without going over. This is where the Bank of Japan may have intervened last week. In the UK...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: BoC tone tilts mildly hawkish – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists note the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) April Summary of Deliberations balanced US trade risks with inflation concerns.

articles.title: Kazaks: ECB will continue to decide meeting by meeting, based on incoming data
articles.expand: The European Central Bank Governing Council member Martins Kazaks said in a presentation during an event on Thursday, the central bank will continue to decide meeting by meeting and on the basis of incoming data.

articles.title: China new bank loans disappoint in April as sluggish start to the year continues
articles.expand: New yuan loans for the month of April came in at -¥10 billion. Yes, that's another negative reading and one to follow up the supposed blip from July last year. articles.title: British Pound hovers ahead of UK GDP data, awaits Trump-Xi meeting updates
articles.expand: GBP/USD holds ground following three days of losses, trading around 1.3520 during the Asian hours on Thursday. Traders await the preliminary UK Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the first quarter of 2026, along with Industrial and Manufacturing Production data due later in the day.

articles.title: Who Is Kevin Warsh? Trump's Pick Confirmed As New Federal Reserve Chair After Narrow Senate Vote
articles.expand: Kevin Warsh, confirmed as the next Federal Reserve chair on Wednesday, is a former Wall Street banker and the youngest Fed governor in US history. The 55-year-old worked closely with Ben Bernanke during the 2008 financial crisis and later joined Stanford’s Hoover Institution. Warsh is also linked to the billionaire Lauder family through his wife Ja...

articles.title: War turmoil drains Asian reserves with India, Philippines among hardest hit
articles.expand: Philippines has led losses with its stockpile sinking 8.1 per cent since the conflict started to $104 billion, while India's dropped 5.2 per cent to $691 billion

articles.title: Petrol, Diesel Price Hike in India is Likely if West Asia War Continues, Says RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra
articles.expand: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said India may eventually have to raise petrol and diesel prices if the Middle East crisis continues for a prolonged period. Speaking in Switzerland, Malhotra warned that higher crude oil prices and disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz are increasing pressure on inflation, imports and the rupee, even as the governmen...

articles.title: Japan weighs supplementary budget to shield households from fuel price surge
articles.expand: Japan's government is considering a supplementary budget to ease household fuel costs ahead of summer, Kyodo reports, sending 30- and 40-year JGB yields higher on increased debt issuance fears. Summary: Japan's government is considering a supplementary budget aimed at reducing the burden of rising fuel costs on households ahead of the summer peak-d...

articles.title: ECB’s Lane Lays Out Case for June Hike Amid Global Energy Shock
articles.expand: ECB Chief Economist Philip Lane signaled on Wednesday that the Iran-related oil shock may require ECB tightening to prevent higher energy prices from spreading into wages, inflation expectations, and broader consumer prices. Speaking in London, Lane laid out the intellectual case for a likely June rate hike while acknowledging the Eurozone economy ...

articles.title: BoE’s Mann Warns Bond Market Fragility Matters for Future Rate Hikes
articles.expand: Bank of England policymaker Catherine Mann warned that future interest rate hikes could trigger larger-than-intended tightening in UK financial conditions because of growing fragility in Britain’s gilt market. Speaking on Wednesday, Mann said the increasing role of hedge funds and overseas investors has made the market more sensitive to sudden shif...

articles.title: BOE Mann : Recent geopolitical events have reinforced how exposed the UK economy is
articles.expand: BOE Monetary Policy Committee member Mann is speaking and says: Recent geopolitical events have reinforced how exposed the UK economy is to international shocks that require a policy response. Implications of new sources of finance for the UK current account deficit are not simple. A tighter policy stance could trigger volatility as new actors unwi...

articles.title: USD/JPY Continues to Climb Amid External and Domestic Pressures
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex USD/JPY rose to 157.65 on Wednesday, marking a third consecutive day of gains. The yen came under pressure following stronger-than-expected US inflation data, reinforcing expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain its hawkish stance. Market focus remains on the Bank of Japan. Following its April meeting, ...

articles.title: ECBs Kocher: A June hike is not a baseline for the ECB
articles.expand: ECBs Kocher on the wires saying: A June hike is not a baseline for the ECB Says that the ECB decides on a meeting by meeting basis. A 50 bps hike is very unlikely The market is for a series of hikes going into year-end. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

articles.title: USD/JPY Analysis for May 13, 2026: Bulls Test Resistance as Intervention Risk Lingers
articles.expand: With the Fed frozen by inflation and the BOJ getting serious about rate hikes, USD/JPY is caught in the middle of a policy tug-of-war.

articles.title: New Zealand Inflation Expectations Jump as RBNZ OCR Outlook Turns Hawkish
articles.expand: New Zealand’s latest RBNZ Survey of Expectations showed a sharp rise in near-term inflation expectations, reinforcing concerns that price pressures may remain elevated even as growth slows. articles.title: OECD Sees BoJ Raising Rates to 2% by End-2027
articles.expand: The OECD projected on Wednesday that the Bank of Japan will continue steadily normalizing monetary policy, with the short-term policy rate expected to rise from the current 0.75% to 2% by the end of 2027. In its latest Economic Survey of Japan, the OECD argued that resilient domestic demand, sustained wage growth, and improving inflation […] ...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Minor hurdle comes at 0.7280
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, prompting AUD/USD to confront the area of multi-year peaks. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated domestic inflation and the RBA’s cautious approach.

articles.title: AUDUSD finds support near 100 hour MA. Staying above keeps the buyers in control
articles.expand: The AUDUSD initially dipped during the Asia-Pacific session, but buyers stepped in against a key swing area between 0.7221 and 0.7227. That zone had acted as a ceiling from mid-April through early May before the pair finally broke higher. Since then, the price has traded back and forth around the area, with the level alternating between support and...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.expand: The USDCHF continues to lean against its 100-hour moving average as a key resistance barometer. Corrective rallies last week stalled against that moving average on Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday — and today’s rally attempt once again failed near the same level.
